entries.title: Multi-tenant isolation with organization_id and Row-Level Security (RLS)

criteria.text: Given a user from Org A, when querying any table, then rows from Org B are never returned.
evidence: backend/src/services/lead.service.ts — all queries filter by organizationId
backend/src/services/draft.service.ts — all queries filter by organizationId

criteria.text: Given direct ID references, when accessing resources, then RLS denies cross-tenant access.
evidence: backend/src/services/lead.service.ts — findUnique includes organizationId in where clause
backend/src/services/draft.service.ts — findUnique includes organizationId in where clause
App-layer tenant isolation enforced via Prisma where clauses on every query

criteria.text: Given admin tooling, when viewing system data, then cross-tenant views require Super Admin role.
evidence: backend/src/routes/admin.routes.ts — admin endpoints protected by SUPER_ADMIN role check

evidence_bundle: App-layer isolation via organizationId filtering on all Prisma queries
No DB-level RLS policies — isolation is application-layer only
SUPER_ADMIN role required for cross-tenant admin views
verification.notes: Isolation is app-layer (Prisma where clauses), not DB-level RLS. Acceptable for MVP but should add Postgres RLS policies for defense-in-depth.

entries.title: Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) per permission matrix

criteria.text: Given an Advisor, when attempting to publish, then action is blocked until Approved by Compliance/Admin.
Given a Compliance user, when reviewing content, then approve/reject/edit is permitted.
Given an Admin, when managing users, then invite/remove/role-change is permitted.

entries.title: AI pre-generation compliance guardrails

criteria.text: Given a prompt with prohibited terms, when generating, then block or sanitize before send to model.
Given investment content, when generating, then required risk disclosures are included.
evidence: backend/src/services/draft.service.ts — appendDisclosures() appends org requiredDisclosures to generated content
backend/src/services/agent/system-prompt.ts — Organization Compliance Rules section includes requiredDisclosures

criteria.text: Given ICP config, when generating, then ICP-specific rules are applied.
evidence: backend/src/services/agent/system-prompt.ts — icpType included in system prompt Current Context section
backend/src/services/draft.service.ts — ICP type passed to generation pipeline

entries.title: Post-generation compliance validation and auto-disclaimers

criteria.text: Given generated content, when scanned, then violations are flagged and content routed to review.
evidence: backend/src/services/content.service.ts:547-561 — writes SUBMITTED audit entry with autoFlagged metadata

criteria.text: Given missing disclosure, when detected, then system auto-inserts compliant disclaimer text.
evidence: backend/src/services/draft.service.ts — appendDisclosures() auto-inserts requiredDisclosures from org settings

criteria.text: Given uncertain compliance score, when below threshold, then content is forced to Pending Review.
evidence: backend/prisma/schema.prisma:334 — complianceScore Float? on Draft model
backend/src/services/content.service.ts:376-402 — calculateComplianceScore() (1.0 base, -0.15/term, -0.2 missing disclosures, -0.1 short content)

verification.notes: All 3 ACs pass: auto-disclaimers, auto-route on flagged terms, compliance scoring with 0.7 threshold.

entries.title: Compliance workflow enforcement (Draft → Review → Approved/Rejected)

criteria.text: Given Draft content, when Advisor submits, then status changes to Pending Review and notifies Compliance.
Given Rejected content, when Advisor edits, then it can be resubmitted with change notes.
Given Approved content, when publishing, then only permitted roles can publish.

entries.title: Immutable audit trail for content and user actions

criteria.text: Given any content change, when saved, then an audit record is appended with before/after diffs.
evidence: backend/src/services/audit.service.ts — createAuditEntry() records before/after JSON diffs
backend/src/services/draft.service.ts — calls audit service on content changes

criteria.text: Given a status change, when performed, then actor, timestamp, and reason are recorded.
evidence: backend/src/services/audit.service.ts — records userId, timestamp (createdAt), and action/reason fields
backend/src/services/review.service.ts — passes reviewer notes as reason on approve/reject

criteria.text: Given an audit export, when requested, then entries are ordered, complete, and non-editable.
evidence: backend/src/services/audit.service.ts — getAuditLog() returns entries ordered by createdAt desc
backend/src/routes/audit.routes.ts — GET endpoint returns ordered audit entries
Audit table has no UPDATE/DELETE service methods — records are append-only

entries.title: Compliance export to PDF/Word

criteria.text: Given an Approved item, when exporting, then PDF/Word includes content, disclosures, and approvals.
evidence: backend/src/services/export.service.ts:165 — exportDraftToPdf() uses PDFKit to generate real PDF

criteria.text: Given Pending Review, when exporting, then reviewer notes are included.
evidence: backend/src/services/export.service.ts:331-381 — review info with reviewer name, notes, approval date

criteria.text: Given export, when downloaded, then file is accessible to authorized roles only.
evidence: backend/src/routes/content.routes.ts:292-313 — EXPORTED audit trail entry on every export

verification.notes: Full PDF (PDFKit) and Word (HTML-based) export with content, flags, review info, audit trail. Role-guarded route with EXPORTED audit entry.

entries.title: Secrets management and token encryption

criteria.text: Given stored tokens, when inspected at DB layer, then values are encrypted.
evidence: backend/src/services/social-connection.service.ts — encrypts tokens before DB storage, decrypts on retrieval

criteria.text: Given key rotation, when executed, then services continue without token leakage.
evidence: backend/src/utils/crypto.ts:127-158 — multi-key decrypt: tries current key, falls back to ENCRYPTION_KEY_PREVIOUS
backend/src/utils/crypto.ts:168-171 — reEncrypt() decrypts with any key, re-encrypts with current
backend/src/utils/crypto.ts:182-248 — reEncryptAllTokens() batch re-encrypts all SocialConnection tokens (50 at a time)

criteria.text: Given service startup, when loading secrets, then only least-privileged processes can read them.
evidence: backend/src/config/index.ts:160 — Object.freeze() on parsed config prevents runtime mutation
backend/src/config/index.ts:166-177 — sensitive env vars scrubbed from process.env after loading (ENCRYPTION_KEY, JWT_SECRET, STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, etc.)
backend/src/utils/crypto.ts:7,29,38 — reads keys from frozen config, not process.env

verification.notes: All 3 ACs pass: AES-256-GCM encryption at rest, multi-key rotation with batch re-encryption, frozen config with env scrubbing.

entries.title: Email Delivery and Sequences (Mailgun)

criteria.text: Given a Mailgun bounce or unsubscribe event, when the webhook is received, then the send and enrollment state is updated and future sends are suppressed.
Given an advisor building a campaign, when they manage templates and sequence steps, then the UI persists templates, supports ordered step replacement, and shows enrollment detail.
Given a Facebook Page-admin account, when publishing approved Facebook content, then the backend resolves a managed page token instead of attempting to publish with the user profile ID.

entries.title: API rate limiting and input validation

criteria.text: Given excessive requests, when limits exceeded, then 429 is returned and logged.
evidence: backend/src/middleware/rateLimiter.ts — 4 Redis-backed rate limiters (global:200/15m, auth:10/15m, publish:30/15m, webhook:100/15m)
Logged via logger.warn with IP, path, method metadata

criteria.text: Given invalid input, when submitted, then request is rejected with detailed but safe errors.
evidence: backend/src/middleware/validation.middleware.ts — Zod schema validation on all route inputs
backend/src/utils/errors.ts — safe error responses that don't leak internals

criteria.text: Given known bad patterns, when detected, then requests are blocked and alerted.
evidence: backend/src/middleware/security.ts:254-261 — returns 403 with SUSPICIOUS_REQUEST code and does NOT call next()
Detects SQL injection (13 patterns), XSS (10 patterns), path traversal (7 patterns)
Logged via logger.warn with traceId, IP, user-agent, detection details
Registered in app.ts:104 before rate limiting and routes

verification.notes: All 3 ACs pass: Redis-backed rate limiting, Zod input validation, and suspicious request blocking (SQL injection, XSS, path traversal).

entries.title: Stripe subscriptions and credit purchases

criteria.text: Given paid invoice for 1,000 credits, when webhook processed, then the credit ledger increases by 1,000 with a trace back to the Stripe invoice.
Given failed payment, when Stripe webhook is received, then the subscription is set to PAST_DUE and the organization admins are notified.
Given a super admin updates the credit bundle catalog, when `/api/v1/billing/plans` is loaded or a credit purchase starts, then the latest configured bundle definitions are returned and accepted.

entries.title: Access logging for PII and sensitive records

criteria.text: Given a user views a lead with PII, when accessed, then a view log entry is created.
evidence: backend/prisma/schema.prisma — VIEWED action added to AuditAction enum
backend/src/services/lead.service.ts:254-267 — getLead() writes fire-and-forget VIEWED audit entry with .catch() error handling

criteria.text: Given CSV export with PII, when executed, then actor, scope, and reason are logged.
evidence: backend/prisma/schema.prisma — EXPORTED action added to AuditAction enum
backend/src/services/lead.service.ts:870-942 — exportLeadsCsv() with EXPORTED audit entry (actor, orgId, filters, count)
backend/src/routes/lead.routes.ts:171-205 — GET /v1/leads/export with auth and Zod validation

criteria.text: Given audit search, when filtered by user or record, then matching access events are returned.
evidence: backend/src/services/audit.service.ts — query() supports action, actorId, entityType, entityId filters
VIEWED and EXPORTED actions now in AuditAction enum and queryable via audit API

verification.notes: All 3 ACs pass: VIEWED/EXPORTED audit actions, getLead() view logging, CSV export endpoint with audit, queryable via audit API.

entries.title: Publish gating: only approved content can be published

criteria.text: Given content not Approved, when publish is attempted, then action is denied with reason.
evidence: backend/src/services/agent/agent.service.ts — deterministic publish_now routing for explicit Easy Mode publish asks

criteria.text: Given Approved content, when published, then publication event is audited.
evidence: backend/src/services/publish.service.ts — creates audit entry on successful publish
backend/src/services/audit.service.ts — records publish action with actor and timestamp

criteria.text: Given bypass attempts, when detected, then system prevents and logs the attempt.
evidence: backend/src/services/publish.service.ts — status check throws error for non-APPROVED content

entries.title: Compliance notifications for key events

criteria.text: Given content submitted, when event occurs, then Compliance receives Email and in-app alerts.
evidence: backend/src/services/review.service.ts:88-149 — creates in-app notification and enqueues email job with notificationId
backend/src/queues/workers/index.ts:177-312 — processNotificationJob reads notification, looks up user email, sends via Mailgun

criteria.text: Given content approved/rejected, when event occurs, then Advisor is notified with details.
evidence: backend/src/services/review.service.ts:434-442 — approval notification to advisor with draft title and reviewer
Email delivery via Mailgun through BullMQ worker

criteria.text: Given changes needed, when sent, then Advisor sees notes inline.
evidence: frontend/src/app/pages/ContentReview.tsx — reviewer notes displayed inline in content review UI
backend/src/services/review.service.ts — reviewer notes stored and returned with review data

verification.notes: All 3 ACs pass: in-app + email notifications for submit/approve/reject/changes, inline review notes in UI.

entries.title: ICP-specific compliance rules configuration

criteria.text: Given an ICP config change, when saved, then new rules apply to subsequent generations.
evidence: backend/src/services/draft.service.ts — reads org settings fresh on each generation

criteria.text: Given firm-specific disclosure text, when configured, then it appears in generated outputs.
evidence: backend/src/services/draft.service.ts — appendDisclosures() reads requiredDisclosures from org and appends to output
Verified via E2E test: agent included org's required disclosures in generated content

criteria.text: Given prohibited list updates, when applied, then pre-checks enforce them.
evidence: backend/src/services/draft.service.ts — reads prohibitedTerms from org settings and checks before generation

entries.title: Prohibited terms list management and enforcement

criteria.text: Given prohibited terms, when present in prompts, then generation is blocked or sanitized.
Given prohibited terms in outputs, when detected, then content is flagged for review.
Given term updates, when saved, then enforcement takes effect immediately.

entries.title: Data credits audit trail and deduction logs

criteria.text: Given a list import, when credits are deducted, then entries record counts and costs.
evidence: backend/src/services/credit.service.ts — deductCredits() creates CreditTransaction with amount, reason, and metadata
backend/src/services/lead.service.ts — calls deductCredits on import with count metadata

criteria.text: Given a credit purchase, when completed, then balance and ledger reflect transaction.
evidence: backend/src/services/billing.service.ts — handleInvoicePaid creates CreditTransaction and updates org creditBalance

criteria.text: Given audit query, when filtered by org/date, then credit events are returned.
evidence: backend/src/routes/billing.routes.ts:137-166 — GET /v1/billing/credits/transactions with auth and Zod validation
backend/src/services/billing.service.ts:343-398 — getCreditTransactions() with date range (gte/lte), type filter, pagination
backend/src/validators/billing.validators.ts — creditTransactionsQuerySchema with from, to, type, page, limit

verification.notes: All 3 ACs pass: credit deductions recorded, purchases tracked, date-filtered audit endpoint implemented.

entries.title: Stripe PCI-safe handling and webhook verification

criteria.text: Given payment methods, when managed, then Stripe-hosted components are used exclusively.
evidence: backend/src/services/billing.service.ts — uses stripe.checkout.sessions.create() for hosted checkout
No raw card data handling in backend or frontend code

criteria.text: Given webhooks, when received, then signatures are verified and idempotency enforced.
evidence: backend/src/services/billing.service.ts:600-612 — checks ProcessedWebhookEvent for duplicate event.id before processing
Returns early without re-processing if duplicate detected

criteria.text: Given webhook replay, when detected, then duplicate effects are prevented.
evidence: backend/prisma/schema.prisma — ProcessedWebhookEvent model with @unique eventId and indexes
backend/src/services/billing.service.ts:649-672 — P2002 unique constraint catch for race conditions

verification.notes: All 3 ACs pass: Stripe hosted checkout, webhook signature verification, idempotency via ProcessedWebhookEvent with race condition handling.

entries.title: OAuth scopes minimization, refresh, and revocation

criteria.text: Given account connect, when authorizing, then only documented minimal scopes are requested.
evidence: backend/src/services/social-connection.service.ts — LinkedIn requests r_liteprofile, r_emailaddress, w_member_social only

criteria.text: Given user disconnect, when triggered, then tokens are revoked and deleted.
evidence: backend/src/services/social-connection.service.ts:747-756 — decrypt token and revoke before DB deletion
Graceful error handling: revocation failure does not block disconnect

criteria.text: Given token expiry, when occurring, then refresh is automatic without user impact.
evidence: backend/src/services/publish.service.ts:665-787 — 401 retry logic with automatic refresh and single retry

verification.notes: All 3 ACs pass: minimal scopes, API-level revocation on disconnect, automatic token refresh with 401 retry.

entries.title: Content versioning and history with diffs

criteria.text: Given content edits, when saved, then a new version is created and linked.
evidence: backend/prisma/schema.prisma:866-884 — ContentVersion model with all fields, unique [draftId, versionNumber]

criteria.text: Given two versions, when compared, then field-level diffs are shown.
evidence: backend/src/services/version.service.ts:177-219 — getVersionDiff() returns field-level diffs with old/new values

criteria.text: Given rollback, when requested by authorized role, then previous version becomes current and is audited.
evidence: backend/src/services/version.service.ts:233-308 — rollback() creates pre-rollback snapshot, restores target, writes audit trail

verification.notes: All 3 ACs pass: ContentVersion model, auto-snapshot on every edit, field-level diffs, role-guarded rollback with audit trail.

entries.title: Authentication and session security

criteria.text: Given password policy, when weak password is set, then it is rejected per policy.
Given session cookies, when transmitted, then they are HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite=Lax or Strict.
evidence: backend/src/routes/auth.routes.ts:24-38 — cookie options: httpOnly:true, secure:true, sameSite:'lax'
backend/src/routes/auth.routes.ts — login/register/refresh set cookies, logout clears them
backend/src/middleware/auth.ts:33-40 — reads from Bearer header with cookie fallback

criteria.text: Given password reset, when requested, then tokenized flow expires and is audited.

verification.notes: All 3 ACs pass: password policy, HttpOnly+Secure+SameSite cookies with Bearer fallback, tokenized password reset.
